---
title: "Machine Learning Marathon"
listing:
  sort-ui: false
  sort:
    - "date desc"
  type: default
  image-height: 150px
  fields: [image, author, title, description, date, categories]

  categories: true
  date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
  page-size: 6
  contents:
  - "./*.qmd"
  - "!./index.qmd"
toc: false
toc-location: body
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---
The [Machine Learning Marathon (MLM)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/) is an annual 12-week ML/AI hackathon hosted by the [ML+X](https://datascience.wisc.edu/ml-community/) community at UW-Madison. Each fall, teams of 2-5 tackle real-world challenges sourced from UW researchers, industry partners, and the broader Kaggle community. Challenges span a range of domains and difficulty levels -- from beginner-friendly "Getting Started" projects to competitive "Active" challenges with prizes.

Interested in proposing a challenge or participating? Visit [ml-marathon.wisc.edu](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/) or reach out to [Chris Endemann](mailto:endemann@wisc.edu).

### Explore past challenges
